expectations.text: Code is inside the DooleyBuildingSolutions::HouseShellBuilder module namespace
expectations.evidence: module DooleyBuildingSolutions / module HouseShellBuilder wraps all code

expectations.text: model.start_operation and model.commit_operation present, with rescue block calling model.abort_operation
expectations.evidence: model.start_operation('Draw Crawl Space Foundation', true) and model.commit_operation shown; rescue calls model.abort_operation

expectations.text: rescue clause catches a specific exception class (e.g. Sketchup::Error, StandardError) — NOT a bare rescue
expectations.evidence: Three separate rescue clauses: Sketchup::Error, ArgumentError, StandardError — no bare rescue

expectations.text: @materials_cache hash pattern used — material is not created anew on every call
expectations.evidence: @materials_cache ||= {} with ||= assignment pattern in cached_material helper

expectations.text: apply_bim_data called with 'Footing' type for BIM attribution
expectations.evidence: apply_bim_data.call(grp, 'Footing') called in draw_wall_sub for every segment group

expectations.text: No use of eval, instance_eval, or shell-out (system/backticks)
expectations.evidence: No eval, instance_eval, system, backticks, or %x{} anywhere in the code

expectations.text: Individual method bodies are under 20 lines (long logic extracted into helpers)
expectations.evidence: Main method orchestrates helpers; compute_vent_intervals, draw_crawl_segment, draw_vent_surround, draw_wall_sub all under 20 lines each
